extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. Transition t4 ("on send_attempt if (opted_out and acc == 28): sent := true") explicitly sets sent=true while opted_out is true, provided acc==28. So the property fails iff a reachable state has opted_out=true and acc==28 when a send_attempt fires.

The acc update functions are modular: step_0 = (3*acc+12) mod 32, step_1 = (3*acc+31) mod 32, step_2 = (3*acc+15) mod 32 (the nested if-then-else subtracting 32 up to 3 times is just mod-32 reduction; max pre-reduction value 3*31+31=124 needs at most 3 subtractions, which the 3-level structure provides). acc is changed only by step events; optout and send transitions leave acc unchanged.

Starting from acc=0, step_1 gives (3*0+31) mod 32 = 31; another step_1 gives (3*31+31) mod 32 = 124 mod 32 = 28. So two step_1 events reach acc=28 (acc stays in [0,31]). Then optout (t1) sets opted_out=true, sent=false, acc unchanged at 28. Then send_attempt: guards t2 (not opted_out) fails, t3 (opted_out and acc!=28) fails since acc==28, t4 (opted_out and acc==28) holds and sets sent:=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true, sent=true, acc=28 — violating opted_out implies not sent.

Counterexample execution (4 events, well within the 34-event bound), verified by simulation:
init (opted_out=F, sent=F, acc=0)
step_1 -> (F, F, 31)
step_1 -> (F, F, 28)
optout -> (T, F, 28)
send_attempt -> (T, T, 28)  <-- opted_out=true and sent=true: VIOLATION.
